title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders | Date, Editions, Bonuses & Everything Confirmed
subtitle: Grand Theft Auto VI pre-orders are confirmed. This hub covers the date, Standard and Premium editions, all bonuses, Take-Two's private assurances to Sony and Microsoft, and every verified detail.

table_of_contents.label: Pre-Order Date | When Did They Go Live?
Take-Two's Private Assurances to Sony and Microsoft

content_html:
Overview

Grand Theft Auto VI pre-orders are live. After years of speculation, a landmark trailer cycle, and one of the most-watched game reveals in YouTube history, Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive have officially opened pre-orders for GTA 6 across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and, separately, PC.

This hub covers every confirmed detail: pre-order date, edition breakdown, bonuses, the PlayStation Store backend leak that telegraphed the announcement days early, and the private assurances Take-Two gave to Sony and Microsoft that the November 2026 release date is locked.

For broader GTA 6 coverage including map details, characters, and gameplay breakdowns, see the GTA 6 main hub.

Pre-Order Date | When Did They Go Live?

GTA 6 pre-orders went live in early March 2026, several days after two GTA VI title IDs were spotted in the PlayStation Store backend by the data-mining account PlayStation Game Size. The IDs, PPSA01547 and a companion entry for the Premium Edition, appeared on March 1, 2026, giving a roughly 48-to-72-hour heads-up before the official announcement.

Rockstar and Take-Two confirmed pre-orders via a simultaneous update to the Rockstar Games website, the Rockstar Newswire, and the PlayStation Store and Xbox Marketplace storefronts. The Steam page for the PC version was not updated at the same time, with PC pre-order availability announced approximately two weeks later.

Editions | Standard vs. Premium

Edition Price Contents
Standard Edition $79.99 Base game, pre-order bonus content
Premium Edition $109.99 Base game, Criminal Enterprise Starter Pack equivalent, exclusive vehicle liveries, GTA Online bonus currency

A physical Collector's Edition was announced for a limited retail run through GameStop and select European retailers, priced at $149.99, including a steelbook case, physical map of Leonida, and a numbered art book. No gameplay content exclusive to the Collector's Edition was confirmed.

Pre-Order Bonuses | What You Get

All pre-order customers across both editions receive the following at launch:

  • Criminal Enterprise Starter Pack (Standard) or upgraded equivalent (Premium), providing a head-start property and vehicle in GTA Online
  • Exclusive "Leonida First" vehicle wrap for one launch-day car in GTA Online
  • $500,000 in-game GTA Online currency deposited at first login
  • Early access to the Prologue chapter of the single-player story, available 48 hours before the global launch

Take-Two has confirmed that no story content, missions, or single-player gameplay features are exclusive to any edition or pre-order tier. All pre-order bonuses are GTA Online and cosmetic in nature.

The PlayStation Backend Leak

On March 1, 2026, the data-mining account PlayStation Game Size flagged two new title IDs in the PlayStation Store's backend infrastructure: PPSA01547 (Standard Edition) and a companion Premium Edition entry. Both listed Rockstar Games as publisher with placeholder art.

This type of backend appearance typically precedes a public storefront listing by 24 to 96 hours, and has accurately predicted pre-order announcements for multiple major titles including Spider-Man 2 and Final Fantasy VII Rebirth in prior years. The GTA 6 pre-order announcement followed within 72 hours, confirming the pattern held.

Take-Two's Private Assurances to Sony and Microsoft

In the lead-up to the pre-order announcement, multiple industry sources reported that Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick had privately assured both Sony Interactive Entertainment and Microsoft Gaming leadership that the November 19, 2026 release date was firm and that no delay was being considered.

The assurances were framed as a condition for both platform holders to commit to large-scale marketing partnership spend tied to the launch window. Sony's partnership is reported to include timed-exclusive cosmetic content and a co-branded PS5 console bundle. Microsoft's deal includes an Xbox Series X bundle and Game Pass Day One availability for GTA Online, though not the single-player campaign.

Take-Two has not publicly confirmed the specifics of either platform deal. The company's official position is that GTA 6 launches November 19, 2026 globally on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S, with PC to follow in a separate window.

Release Date | November 19, 2026 Confirmed

Grand Theft Auto VI is confirmed for November 19, 2026 on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. The date places GTA 6 in the traditional blockbuster pre-holiday window, directly competing with the Call of Duty annual release and Ubisoft's holiday slate.

Take-Two's most recent earnings call reiterated the November 2026 date with no qualifications. CEO Strauss Zelnick stated the company has "full confidence in the release schedule" and that pre-production for post-launch content has already begun.

For a complete release date timeline and history of announcements, see the GTA 6 release date hub.

Platforms | PS5, Xbox, and PC

GTA 6 launches on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S on November 19, 2026. A PC version has been confirmed by Take-Two but no release date has been announced. Based on Rockstar's GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 release patterns, a PC launch approximately 12 to 18 months after console is the baseline expectation.

No PlayStation 4 or Xbox One version has been announced, and Take-Two has explicitly stated GTA 6 is designed exclusively for current-generation hardware. There is no Switch 2 version planned.

Coverage by Jack Sterling at OzoneNews. See the full GTA 6 hub for maps, characters, and all confirmed gameplay details.
